note: Hindu Marriage Act guardianship vs JJ Act adoption - JJ Act provides secular adoption framework that supersedes HMA for adoption matters
Divorce under HMA Section 13 may conflict with right to residence under DV Act Section 17 - married woman retains residence rights in shared household even during divorce proceedings
Maintenance under CrPC Section 125 and DV Act Section 20 can be claimed concurrently - DV Act provides additional monetary relief beyond CrPC maintenance

Restitution of conjugal rights under HMA Section 9 may conflict with protection orders under DV Act Section 18 - woman's safety and protection from domestic violence takes precedence over conjugal rights
Hindu Adoptions and Maintenance Act adoption provisions vs JJ Act - JJ Act allows inter-religion adoption while HAMA restricts to Hindus; JJ Act prevails for secular adoption
Maintenance of wife under HAMA Section 18 vs CrPC Section 125 - both provisions applicable concurrently; CrPC provides quicker remedy while HAMA provides comprehensive maintenance
Hindu Minority and Guardianship Act natural guardian rules vs JJ Act adoption - child welfare principle under JJ Act prevails over traditional guardianship rules

Muslim Women (Protection of Rights on Divorce) Act Section 3 maintenance vs CrPC Section 125 - Supreme Court in Danial Latifi held both applicable; husband must provide reasonable maintenance during iddat and beyond
POCSO Section 29 presumption of guilt vs CrPC Section 167 bail provisions - POCSO's stringent bail conditions under Section 29 apply over general CrPC bail provisions
Enforcement of protection orders under DV Act Section 31 vs maintenance enforcement under CrPC - both remedies available simultaneously; breach of protection order is cognizable offence
Void marriage provisions differ between HMA Section 11 and Special Marriage Act Section 24 - applicable law depends on whether marriage was solemnized under Hindu law or registered under SMA

Property succession rules differ for Hindu women under HSA Section 14 vs Christian women under ISA Section 15 - personal law determines applicable succession regime; Hindu women have absolute ownership
Definition of domestic violence under DV Act Section 2 vs cruelty under IPC Section 498A - DV Act has broader definition including economic abuse; both provisions can be invoked simultaneously
HMA Section 5 marriageable age (18 for bride) vs POCSO Section 2 definition of child (below 18) - sexual intercourse with wife aged 15-18 may constitute rape under POCSO despite valid marriage
General FIR registration under CrPC Section 154 vs mandatory reporting under POCSO Section 19 - POCSO imposes stricter timeline and penalties for failure to report child sexual abuse
Application to Magistrate under DV Act Section 12 vs complaint procedure under CrPC Section 200 - DV Act provides quicker relief without formal complaint procedure
Remarriage rights under HMA Section 15 vs property rights under HSA Section 14 - divorced woman's property rights may be affected by remarriage timing; appeal period considerations apply
JJ Act Section 74 prohibition on disclosing child identity vs RTI Act Section 8 exemptions - child identity absolutely protected even under RTI; no balancing test required
POCSO Section 33 compensation powers vs CrPC Section 357 compensation - POCSO provides enhanced compensation under Section 33(8); both provisions can be used for victim compensation
DV Act Section 26 right to seek relief in other proceedings vs CrPC Section 125 - woman can seek DV Act reliefs in pending CrPC proceedings; no separate application needed
Maintenance pendente lite under HMA Section 24 vs DV Act Section 20 monetary relief - DV Act provides broader relief including loss of earnings; both can be claimed but not duplicate amounts
Conditions of marriage under SMA Section 4 vs HMA Section 5 - SMA applies to inter-faith marriages; HMA applies to Hindus; conflicting provisions resolved by determining applicable personal law
Anticipatory bail under CrPC Section 438 vs POCSO Section 29 presumption - courts apply stricter scrutiny for POCSO cases; anticipatory bail rarely granted in POCSO matters
DV Act Section 19 residence orders vs Transfer of Property Act Section 6 - Magistrate can restrain disposal of shared household despite general property transfer rights under TPA
HSA Section 6 coparcenary rights vs Benami Transactions Act Section 3 - property held benami cannot be claimed as coparcenary property; Benami Act overrides succession claims
